description_long: This is a wooden baseball bat with a cylindrical shape, tapering from a thick barrel to a narrower handle. The barrel is covered with a layer of barbed wire, giving it a rugged and menacing appearance, often associated with improvised or weaponized bats. The wood is a medium brown color, and the barbed wire is metallic and tightly wrapped around the upper portion of the bat. The handle is smooth and ends in a rounded knob, which is free of any additional wrapping or tape. There are no visible logos, markings, or manufacturer details on the bat. The overall finish is matte, with the barbed wire adding a rough, grippy texture to the barrel.
description: A wooden baseball bat with a barbed wire-wrapped barrel and a smooth, rounded handle. The bat has a rugged, matte finish with no visible logos or markings.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the bat, with the barbed wire-wrapped barrel and smooth handle clearly visible.
description_view_1: End-on view of the barrel, highlighting the circular cross-section and dense barbed wire wrapping.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the full length and the transition from the barbed wire barrel to the plain handle.
description_view_3: Handle-end view, displaying the rounded knob and the tapering handle leading up to the barbed wire section.
attribute_end_cap_design: The end cap is a simple rounded wooden knob, matching the handle in color and material, with no distinct markings or logos.
